page.title: Template:Climate chart/How to read a climate chart

revision.comment: Imported from wikipedia at 2025-05-11 01:42:51 on [[Template:Climate chart/How to read a climate chart]]
Include imperial fields.
Line seems to be gray (#CBA), not red.
[[Help:Reverting|Reverted]] edits by [[Special:Contributions/Matramas|Matramas]] ([[User talk:Matramas|talk]]) to last version by 193.77.151.137

revision.text:
The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the amount of [[precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32°F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-7|-2|30 |-7|-1|34 |-3|4|54 |1|9|68 |7|16|97 |11|20|123 |12|22|116 |11|20|118 |8|15|100 |4|9|116 |-2|3|107 |-6|0|84 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cuzco|Cuzco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{-}}
As we can see from the chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with hot summers and freezing winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies in the heart of the tropics, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cuzco also lies near the equator, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cuzco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
==See also== * [[Climograph]]

The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the amount of [[precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32°F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Port Sophie]] |15|20|21| |15|21|28| |16|22|76| |17|23|113| |20|27|154| |22|29|191| |23|30|204| |24|31|188| |22|29|130| |20|27|97| |18|24|45| |16|21|19| |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Canna]] |12|20|23 |13|21|19 |15|22|8 |17|24|2 |19|26|0 |20|26|0 |22|27|0 |21|27|0 |20|26|5 |17|23|12 |15|22|25 |13|21|31 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cuzco|Cuzco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{-}}
As we can see from the chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with hot summers and freezing winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies in the heart of the tropics, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cuzco also lies near the equator, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cuzco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
==See also== * [[Climograph]]

revision.comment: /* See also */
revision.text:
The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the amount of [[precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32°F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-7|-2|30 |-7|-1|34 |-3|4|54 |1|9|68 |7|16|97 |11|20|123 |12|22|116 |11|20|118 |8|15|100 |4|9|116 |-2|3|107 |-6|0|84 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cuzco|Cuzco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{-}}
As we can see from the chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with hot summers and freezing winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies in the heart of the tropics, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cuzco also lies near the equator, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cuzco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
==See also== * [[Climograph]] hi

revision.comment: /* See also */
revision.text:
The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the amount of [[precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32°F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-7|-2|30 |-7|-1|34 |-3|4|54 |1|9|68 |7|16|97 |11|20|123 |12|22|116 |11|20|118 |8|15|100 |4|9|116 |-2|3|107 |-6|0|84 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cuzco|Cuzco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{-}}
As we can see from the chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with hot summers and freezing winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies in the heart of the tropics, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cuzco also lies near the equator, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cuzco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
==See also== * [[Climograph]] * [[Climograph and suck its own poop.com]]

revision.comment: Revision
revision.text:
The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the amount of [[precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32°F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-7|-2|30 |-7|-1|34 |-3|4|54 |1|9|68 |7|16|97 |11|20|123 |12|22|116 |11|20|118 |8|15|100 |4|9|116 |-2|3|107 |-6|0|84 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cuzco|Cuzco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{-}}
As we can see from the chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with hot summers and freezing winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies near the equator, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cuzco also lies in the Tropical Zone, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Almost like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cuzco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
==See also== * [[Climograph]]

revision.comment: merge See also link into lead
22 C is definitely not "hot" summer
/* top */clean up, replaced: {{-}} → {{clear}} using [[Project:AWB|AWB]] (12084)
Lol
Fixing.
Added {{See also|}}
Explanation that the "new" style climate charts remove ease of use, data, and overall utility from the older style.
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}} {{See also|Template:Climate chart/doc}} [[Climate chart]]s provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the amount of [[precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32 °F, the point of freezing, for orientation. The best way to read this "new" climate chart is to just go look for the older far more informative and easier to read style, or go to a different website for better more complete data. Unfortunately this "new style" is less and informative and damages the usefulness of one of the best websites in the world. {{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-7|-2|30. |-7|-1|34 |-3|4|54 |1|9|68 |7|16|97 |11|20|123 |12|22|116 |11|20|118 |8|15|100 |4|9|116 |-2|3|107 |-6|0|84 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cusco|Cusco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{clear}}
As displayed in the accompanying chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with warm summers and chilly winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies near the equator, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cusco also lies in the Tropical Zone, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Almost like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cusco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.

revision.comment: added the word "average" to the sentence "The blue bars represent the average amount of [[precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month."
Changed the first climate chart, as the data is inaccurate. Figures from the Slovenian Environment Agency.
reverted vandalism
Bot: convert local/ILL link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}} {{See also|Template:Climate chart/doc}} [[:en:Climate chart|Climate chart]]s provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the average amount of [[:en:Precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32 °F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-4|4|33 |-3|7|43 |1|12|49 |5|17|62 |10|21|97 |13|25|107 |15|27|103 |14|27|101 |10|21|113 |6|16|87 |2|10|82 |-3|5|60 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cusco|Cusco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{clear}}
As displayed in the accompanying chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with warm summers and chilly winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies near the equator, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cusco also lies in the Tropical Zone, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Almost like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cusco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}} {{See also|Template:Climate chart/doc}} [[:en:Climate chart|Climate chart]]s provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the average amount of [[:en:Precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32 °F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-4|4|33 |-3|7|43 |1|12|49 |5|17|62 |10|21|97 |13|25|107 |15|27|103 |14|27|101 |10|21|113 |6|16|87 |2|10|82 |-3|5|60 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cusco|Cusco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{clear}}
As displayed in the accompanying chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with warm summers and chilly winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies near the equator, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cusco also lies in the Tropical Zone, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Almost like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cusco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: add [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] (no interwikis found)
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}} {{See also|Template:Climate chart/doc}} [[:en:Climate chart|Climate chart]]s provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the average amount of [[:en:Precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32 °F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-4|4|33 |-3|7|43 |1|12|49 |5|17|62 |10|21|97 |13|25|107 |15|27|103 |14|27|101 |10|21|113 |6|16|87 |2|10|82 |-3|5|60 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cusco|Cusco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{clear}}
As displayed in the accompanying chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with warm summers and chilly winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies near the equator, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cusco also lies in the Tropical Zone, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Almost like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cusco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}} {{See also|Template:Climate chart/doc}} [[:en:Climate chart|Climate chart]]s provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the average amount of [[:en:Precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32 °F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-4|4|33 |-3|7|43 |1|12|49 |5|17|62 |10|21|97 |13|25|107 |15|27|103 |14|27|101 |10|21|113 |6|16|87 |2|10|82 |-3|5|60 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cusco|Cusco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{clear}}
As displayed in the accompanying chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with warm summers and chilly winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies near the equator, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cusco also lies in the Tropical Zone, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Almost like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cusco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]</noinclude> <noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}} {{See also|Template:Climate chart/doc}} [[:en:Climate chart|Climate chart]]s provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the average amount of [[:en:Precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32 °F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-4|4|33 |-3|7|43 |1|12|49 |5|17|62 |10|21|97 |13|25|107 |15|27|103 |14|27|101 |10|21|113 |6|16|87 |2|10|82 |-3|5|60 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cusco|Cusco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{clear}}
As displayed in the accompanying chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with warm summers and chilly winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies near the equator, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cusco also lies in the Tropical Zone, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Almost like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cusco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}} {{See also|Template:Climate chart/doc}} [[:en:Climate chart|Climate chart]]s provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the average amount of [[:en:Precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32 °F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-4|4|33 |-3|7|43 |1|12|49 |5|17|62 |10|21|97 |13|25|107 |15|27|103 |14|27|101 |10|21|113 |6|16|87 |2|10|82 |-3|5|60 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cusco|Cusco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{clear}}
As displayed in the accompanying chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with warm summers and chilly winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies near the equator, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cusco also lies in the Tropical Zone, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Almost like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cusco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]</noinclude> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23176857634|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}} {{See also|Template:Climate chart/doc}} [[:en:Climate chart|Climate chart]]s provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the average amount of [[:en:Precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32 °F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-4|4|33 |-3|7|43 |1|12|49 |5|17|62 |10|21|97 |13|25|107 |15|27|103 |14|27|101 |10|21|113 |6|16|87 |2|10|82 |-3|5|60 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cusco|Cusco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{clear}}
As displayed in the accompanying chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with warm summers and chilly winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies near the equator, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cusco also lies in the Tropical Zone, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Almost like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cusco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]</noinclude> <noinclude> [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: Text replacement - "</noinclude>↵<noinclude>" to ""
revision.text:
{{Wikipedia how-to}} {{See also|Template:Climate chart/doc}} [[:en:Climate chart|Climate chart]]s provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the average amount of [[:en:Precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32 °F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-4|4|33 |-3|7|43 |1|12|49 |5|17|62 |10|21|97 |13|25|107 |15|27|103 |14|27|101 |10|21|113 |6|16|87 |2|10|82 |-3|5|60 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cusco|Cusco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{clear}}
As displayed in the accompanying chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with warm summers and chilly winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies near the equator, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cusco also lies in the Tropical Zone, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Almost like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cusco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Climate_chart%2FHow_to_read_a_climate_chart here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!-- History offloaded: due to miraheze stability concerns, the edit history of this page has been offloaded to the XML archive. See https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Climate_chart%2FHow_to_read_a_climate_chart for full history. Last refreshed: [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]] --> {{Wikipedia how-to}} {{See also|Template:Climate chart/doc}} [[:en:Climate chart|Climate chart]]s provide an overview of the climate in a particular place. The letters in the top row stand for months: January, February, etc. The bars and numbers convey the following information: The blue bars represent the average amount of [[:en:Precipitation (meteorology)|precipitation]] (rain, snow etc.) that falls in each month. The blue numbers are the amount of precipitation in either millimeters (liters per square meter) or inches. The red numbers are the average daily high and low temperatures for each month, and the red bars represent the average daily temperature span for each month. The thin gray line is 0 °C or 32 °F, the point of freezing, for orientation.
{{climate chart|[[Maribor|Maribor, Slovenia]] |-4|4|33 |-3|7|43 |1|12|49 |5|17|62 |10|21|97 |13|25|107 |15|27|103 |14|27|101 |10|21|113 |6|16|87 |2|10|82 |-3|5|60 |float=left }} {{Climate chart|[[Labuan|Labuan, Malaysia]] |24|30|112 |24|30|117 |24|31|150 |24|32|297 |24|32|345 |24|31|351 |25|31|318 |24|31|297 |24|31|417 |24|31|465 |24|31|419 |24|30|285 |float=left |clear=none }} {{climate chart|[[Cusco|Cusco, Peru]] |7|20|163 |7|21|150 |7|21|109 |4|22|51 |2|21|15 |1|21|5 |-1|21|5 |1|21|10 |4|22|25 |6|22|67 |6|23|76 |7|22|137 |float=left |clear=none }} {{clear}}
As displayed in the accompanying chart, Maribor has a temperate climate with warm summers and chilly winters. It lies in the northern hemisphere, so the temperatures peak in July and August. The temperature in Labuan, which lies near the equator, hardly changes through the year. Instead of summers and winters, there is a dry season in the beginning of the year, followed by a wet season with high rainfall. Cusco also lies in the Tropical Zone, but at a much higher altitude in the Andean highlands, and also much drier. Almost like in Labuan, the daily high temperature barely changes through the year, but they are significantly cooler due to the altitude. Nights in Cusco are much colder than during the day, especially in the dry months from May to August.
<noinclude> [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>
